method_atom: A stochastic agent-based automaton in which many interacting units exchange a conserved additive quantity through repeated probabilistic pairwise interactions. Each unit carries a fixed heterogeneity parameter that biases its behavior under uncertain outcomes via a generalized (nonextensive, q-indexed) utility/decision rule, so units respond asymmetrically to prospective gains versus losses. The long-term collective redistribution pattern of the exchanged quantity depends on the distribution of this parameter across units and on the rules constraining exchanges, and is characterized analytically and numerically.
object_atom: Economic operators engaging in risky economic transactions, exchanging monetary assets under indebtedness restrictions, whose risk-averse or risk-seeking attitudes shape the resulting distribution of wealth.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the heterogeneous agent-based probabilistic exchange automaton with a q-parameterized generalized-utility decision rule governing behavior under uncertainty; the domain-specific object is risk-sensitive economic operators redistributing assets through transactions.
